wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on the Kling AI Image Generation App project overview. Explain the project's purpose as a Flutter-based mobile application that generates AI images from text prompts, its core functionality, target audience, and key features. Document the technology stack including Flutter SDK, Dart programming language, and integration with Kling AI API. Include both conceptual overviews for beginners and technical details for experienced developers. Use terminology consistent with the codebase. Provide practical examples demonstrating the app's capabilities and user workflow. Document the project's architecture philosophy, design principles, and cross-platform deployment strategy.

wiki_catalogs.name: Android Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for Android implementation of the Kling AI Image Generation App. Document the MainActivity.kt FlutterActivity configuration and its role in the Android entry point. Explain the AndroidManifest.xml configuration including permissions, intent filters, and application metadata. Detail the Gradle build configuration in build.gradle.kts covering dependencies, signing configurations, and build variants. Document the gradle.properties and local.properties setup for build optimization and environment variables. Include the Android-specific Flutter embedding process, native plugin registration, and platform channel communication. Provide step-by-step build instructions for generating APK files, signing configurations, and deployment preparation. Address common Android-specific issues, memory management considerations, and performance optimization techniques for mobile devices.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Image Generation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the AI image generation feature. Explain the complete workflow from text prompt input to image display, including the prompt validation, API request processing, and result handling. Document the state management implementation using the GenerationState enum (idle, loading, success, error) and how it controls UI rendering. Detail the asynchronous task processing mechanism with polling intervals, task status checking, and automatic retries. Include practical examples from the codebase showing the _generateImage method implementation, error handling strategies, and user interaction patterns. Explain the integration with the Kling AI API, request formatting, and response parsing for image URLs.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on getting started with the Kling AI Image Generation App. Provide step-by-step installation instructions for setting up the Flutter development environment, including Flutter SDK installation, IDE configuration, and device setup for Android/iOS. Document the environment configuration process, including API credential setup and environment variable management. Include practical examples of running the application locally, debugging setup, and initial project exploration. Address common setup issues and their solutions. Provide guidance on understanding the project structure and navigating the codebase for first-time contributors.

wiki_catalogs.name: iOS Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for iOS implementation of the Kling AI Image Generation App. Document the AppDelegate.swift configuration and its role in the iOS application lifecycle management. Explain the Info.plist configuration including app permissions, URL schemes, and iOS-specific settings. Detail the storyboard setup covering LaunchScreen.storyboard for initial loading and Main.storyboard for the main application interface. Document the Xcode workspace configuration and project structure for Flutter iOS integration. Include the bridging header setup for Objective-C and Swift interoperability. Provide step-by-step build instructions for generating IPA files, code signing with provisioning profiles, and App Store submission preparation. Address iOS-specific considerations including background processing limitations, memory management, device orientation handling, and iOS version compatibility requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the authentication system using JWT tokens with HS256 algorithm. Explain the token generation process, header and payload structure, signature creation using HMAC-SHA256, and credential management. Document the integration with the Kling AI API authentication requirements, token storage strategies, and security considerations. Include practical examples of JWT token construction, encoding processes, and API request authorization headers. Address token expiration handling, refresh mechanisms, and error scenarios related to authentication failures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the Kling AI Image Generation App's overall system design. Describe the high-level architecture following Clean Architecture principles with clear separation of concerns. Document the layered structure including Presentation Layer (UI widgets and state management), Domain Layer (business logic), Data Layer (API client and networking), and Infrastructure Layer (platform-specific implementations). Explain component interactions, data flows between layers, and integration patterns. Include system context diagrams showing how the app interacts with the Kling AI API. Address the state management approach using enum-based state machine pattern, modular directory structure, and component relationships. Document technical decisions, architectural trade-offs, and design constraints.

wiki_catalogs.name: State Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the state management implementation using the state machine pattern. Document the GenerationState enum definition and its four states (idle, loading, success, error), explaining how each state affects UI rendering and user interactions. Detail the setState() usage patterns, state transitions triggered by user actions and API responses, and the relationship between state changes and widget rebuilds. Include practical examples from the codebase showing state updates during image generation, error handling, and UI feedback mechanisms. Explain the benefits of using enum-based state management for predictable UI behavior and debugging.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Features
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on the core features of the Kling AI Image Generation App. Document the primary functionality of AI image generation from text prompts, including the complete workflow from user input to result display. Explain the authentication system using JWT tokens with HS256 algorithm, credential management, and security measures. Detail the state management implementation using a state machine pattern with idle, loading, success, and error states. Document the asynchronous task processing with polling mechanisms, retry logic with exponential backoff, and error handling strategies. Include practical examples from the actual codebase demonstrating feature implementation, user interaction patterns, and technical execution details.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the Kling AI API integration. Document the HTTP client implementation, request/response handling, and error management strategies. Detail the generateImage() method for initiating image generation tasks, getTaskStatus() method for polling task completion, and the polling mechanism with exponential backoff. Include practical examples of API endpoint usage, request/response schemas, authentication headers, and error handling patterns. Explain the asynchronous task processing workflow, timeout handling, and retry logic implementation. Document the integration with the main application screen and how API responses trigger state changes.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for the Kling AI API integration. Document the RESTful API endpoints, specifically the /v1/images/generations endpoint used for image generation requests. For the POST method, document request schema including prompt text, model parameters, and response format. Document HTTP authentication using JWT tokens, request headers, and response handling. Include practical examples of API requests and responses, error codes, and status messages. Document the polling mechanism for checking generation status, timeout handling, and retry strategies. Address rate limiting, error handling strategies, and best practices for API consumption. Provide debugging techniques and monitoring approaches for API interactions.

wiki_catalogs.name: UI/UX Design System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on the UI/UX design system of the Kling AI Image Generation App. Document the design principles including dark theme implementation, Material Design guidelines adherence, and user interface consistency. Explain the color scheme, typography choices, spacing systems, and visual hierarchy used throughout the application. Detail the component library approach, reusable UI elements, and design tokens. Document the state-based rendering system, progress indicators, loading states, and user feedback mechanisms. Include guidelines for responsive design, accessibility compliance, and cross-platform UI consistency. Address the visual design philosophy, user experience patterns, and interaction design principles.

wiki_catalogs.name: Platform Support
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on platform support for the Kling AI Image Generation App. Document the cross-platform mobile development approach using Flutter framework for Android and iOS deployment. Explain the Android implementation including MainActivity.kt configuration, Gradle build setup, permissions management, and APK generation process. Detail the iOS implementation covering AppDelegate.swift configuration, Xcode project setup, provisioning profiles, and IPA generation for App Store distribution. Document platform-specific considerations, native integration points, and deployment strategies. Include build configuration details, signing requirements, and platform-specific troubleshooting approaches.

wiki_catalogs.name: Configuration Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on configuration management for the Kling AI Image Generation App. Document the Flutter project configuration using pubspec.yaml including dependencies, assets management, and build settings. Explain the environment variable system using env.txt and assets/env.txt for API credentials and service endpoints. Detail the code analysis configuration through analysis_options.yaml including linting rules, coding standards, and quality gates. Document the asset management system, build configuration options, and deployment-specific settings. Include best practices for managing sensitive configuration data, environment-specific overrides, and configuration validation approaches.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Guidelines
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on development guidelines for the Kling AI Image Generation App. Document the code quality standards established through analysis_options.yaml including linting rules, coding conventions, and style guidelines. Explain the testing strategy, development workflow, and contribution processes. Detail the project structure conventions, naming patterns, and architectural principles that guide development. Document the Git workflow, branch management, and code review processes. Include best practices for debugging, performance optimization, and maintaining code quality. Address common development pitfalls and their prevention strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment & Distribution
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on deployment and distribution of the Kling AI Image Generation App. Document the Flutter build process including release builds, optimization flags, and asset bundling. Explain the Android deployment workflow covering APK generation, signing, and Google Play Store submission requirements. Detail the iOS deployment process including IPA generation, App Store Connect setup, and TestFlight distribution. Document continuous integration and automated deployment pipelines, version management, and release coordination. Include monitoring setup, crash reporting, and post-deployment maintenance procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Troubleshooting & FAQ
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on troubleshooting and frequently asked questions for the Kling AI Image Generation App. Document common issues including network connectivity problems, API authentication failures, rate limit handling, and platform-specific build errors. Provide systematic debugging approaches for state management issues, API integration problems, and UI rendering challenges. Include diagnostic techniques for performance issues, memory management problems, and platform-specific compatibility concerns. Document error codes, log analysis strategies, and resolution workflows. Address frequently asked questions about setup, configuration, and usage scenarios with practical solutions and workarounds.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# Kling App: AI Image Generation Flutter Application

## Project Introduction

### Purpose Statement
Kling App is a modern Flutter-based mobile application that provides AI-powered image generation capabilities through the Kling AI API. The application enables users to create stunning visual content by simply describing what they want to see in natural language prompts.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- Deliver a seamless AI image generation experience with intuitive user interface
- Implement robust error handling and retry mechanisms for API interactions
- Provide real-time task status monitoring and progress feedback
- Maintain high-performance networking with efficient retry strategies
- Offer a polished dark-themed user experience inspired by modern design systems

### Target Audience
- Content creators seeking quick visual inspiration
- Social media marketers needing promotional graphics
- Designers requiring rapid prototyping visuals
- General users interested in AI-powered creative tools

## Technical Architecture

The application follows a clean, modular architecture pattern with clear separation of concerns:

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Main Entry Point] --> B[UI Layer]
    B --> C[State Management]
    C --> D[API Client]
    D --> E[Kling AI API]
    
    F[Configuration] --> G[Environment Variables]
    G --> D
    
    H[Theme System] --> B
    I[Network Utils] --> D
```

### Component Breakdown
- **UI Layer**: Material Design-based interface with dark theme
- **State Management**: StatefulWidget with controlled state updates
- **API Layer**: Dedicated client for Kling AI service communication
- **Configuration**: Environment-based credential management
- **Networking**: HTTP client with JWT authentication and retry logic

### Design Patterns
- **Repository Pattern**: Centralized API client abstraction
- **State Machine Pattern**: Generation states (idle, loading, success, error)
- **Factory Pattern**: JWT token generation and API request building
- **Observer Pattern**: State change notifications for UI updates

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points
The application starts from the main entry point where the Flutter app is initialized with custom theming and navigation setup.

**Main Application Entry**: `lib/main.dart`
- Initializes the MaterialApp with dark theme configuration
- Sets up the primary color scheme (Indigo primary color)
- Configures the home screen as the image generation interface

**Platform Activities**: 
- Android: `android/app/src/main/kotlin/com/example/kling_app/MainActivity.kt`
- iOS: `ios/Runner/AppDelegate.swift`

### Core Modules

**API Client Implementation**: `lib/core/network/kling_api_client.dart`
- Implements JWT authentication with HS256 algorithm
- Provides retry logic with exponential backoff for rate limiting
- Handles both synchronous and asynchronous image generation workflows
- Includes comprehensive error handling for network failures and API errors

**Configuration Management**: `assets/env.txt`
- Stores environment-specific API credentials
- Supports runtime configuration loading via flutter_dotenv
- Maintains security through environment-based secret management

### Configuration Approach
The application uses a multi-layered configuration system:

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Environment File] --> B[DotEnv Loader]
    B --> C[Runtime Configuration]
    C --> D[API Client]
    D --> E[Authentication]
    
    F[Build Configuration] --> G[Static Constants]
    G --> D
```

**Build Configuration**: `pubspec.yaml`
- Defines Flutter SDK requirements (^3.8.1)
- Specifies core dependencies (http, crypto, flutter_dotenv)
- Configures asset management for environment files

**Analysis Configuration**: `analysis_options.yaml`
- Enforces Flutter Lints best practices
- Maintains code quality standards
- Prevents common Dart programming pitfalls

### External Dependencies
The project leverages several key dependencies:

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[Flutter SDK] --> B[Material Design]
    C[HTTP Package] --> D[Network Requests]
    E[Crypto Package] --> F[JWT Generation]
    G[DotEnv Package] --> H[Environment Loading]
```

- **http**: Network communication and HTTP requests
- **crypto**: Cryptographic operations for JWT signature generation
- **flutter_dotenv**: Environment variable management
- **cupertino_icons**: iOS-style iconography

### Integration Points
The application integrates with the Kling AI service through a RESTful API:

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as User Interface
    participant S as State Manager
    participant A as API Client
    participant K as Kling AI Service
    
    U->>S: User Input
    S->>A: Generate Image Request
    A->>K: POST /v1/images/generations
    K-->>A: Task ID Response
    loop Polling
        A->>K: GET /v1/images/task/{id}
        K-->>A: Status Update
    end
    A-->>S: Image URL
    S-->>U: Display Result
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview
The application provides a streamlined workflow for AI image generation:

1. **Prompt Input**: Multi-line text field for detailed image descriptions
2. **Real-time Generation**: Asynchronous processing with status updates
3. **Result Display**: High-quality image rendering with responsive layouts
4. **Error Handling**: Comprehensive error states with recovery options
5. **Retry Mechanisms**: Intelligent retry logic for transient failures

### Implementation Highlights

**State Management**: `lib/main.dart`
- Implements a four-state state machine (idle, loading, success, error)
- Provides responsive UI feedback during all operation phases
- Handles user input validation and form submission

**Authentication Flow**: `lib/core/network/kling_api_client.dart`
- Generates time-limited JWT tokens with HS256 signatures
- Implements automatic retry with exponential backoff for rate limiting
- Handles network exceptions and malformed responses gracefully

**Design System**: `DESIGN.md`
- Establishes consistent color palette and typography
- Defines spacing guidelines and component styling
- Documents animation patterns and interaction behaviors

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Idle
    Idle --> Loading: User submits prompt
    Loading --> Success: Task completes successfully
    Loading --> Error: Task fails or timeout
    Success --> Idle: New prompt or reset
    Error --> Loading: Retry attempt
    Error --> Idle: Cancel or reset
```

The application's architecture emphasizes reliability and user experience through:

- **Asynchronous Processing**: Non-blocking image generation with polling
- **Progressive Enhancement**: Rich UI states with appropriate feedback
- **Error Resilience**: Comprehensive error handling and recovery mechanisms
- **Performance Optimization**: Efficient retry logic and resource management

**Sources:**
- [pubspec.yaml](pubspec.yaml)
- [main.dart](lib/main.dart)
- [kling_api_client.dart](lib/core/network/kling_api_client.dart)
- [env.txt](assets/env.txt)
- [MainActivity.kt](android/app/src/main/kotlin/com/example/kling_app/MainActivity.kt)
- [AppDelegate.swift](ios/Runner/AppDelegate.swift)
- [DESIGN.md](DESIGN.md)
- [analysis_options.yaml](analysis_options.yaml)

</blog>
